steps.title: Interpret the function and statement
steps.content:
The function $f(x) = 240,000(1.22)^x$ outputs predicted annual revenue in dollars, where $x$ is years after the company started.

The notation $f(5)$ represents the revenue at year 5 — a snapshot value, not a growth factor.

The statement f(5) is approximately equal to 648,650 means this revenue is approximately \$648,650.

steps.title: Identify the correct choice
steps.content:
This directly matches choice A. We can eliminate the other choices:
  • Choice B refers to cumulative increase from start, not the actual revenue at year 5
  • Choice C claims the revenue is 5 times the prior year, but base 1.22 means 1.22 times the prior year (22% annual growth), not 5
  • Choice D claims 5% annual growth, but the base 1.22 indicates 22% annual growth
That matches choice A — A is correct.
